lineage.Process: Append slovakia_enuts3 ENUTS3 NO_TEST "CNTRY_NAME "CNTRY_NAME" true false false 254 Text 0 0 ,First,#,slovakia_enuts3,CNTRY_NAME,-1,-1;CNTRY_CODE "CNTRY_CODE" true false false 4 Text 0 0 ,First,#;BPL_CODE "BPL_CODE" true false false 19 Double 0 0 ,First,#;ENUTS3 "ENUTS3" true false false 10 Text 0 0 ,First,#;ADMIN_NAME "ADMIN_NAME" true false false 250 Text 0 0 ,First,#,slovakia_enuts3,ADMIN_NAME,-1,-1" #

CopyMultiple "Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts\zipped_shapefiles\ENUTS2.shp shapefile_polygon;Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts\zipped_shapefiles\ENUTS2_2013.shp shapefile_polygon;Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts\zipped_shapefiles\ENUTS3.shp shapefile_polygon;Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts\zipped_shapefiles\ENUTS3_2013.shp shapefile_polygon" C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\enuts ENUTS2;ENUTS2_2013;ENUTS3;ENUTS3_2013 "ENUTS2 FeatureClass ENUTS2.shp #;ENUTS2_2013 FeatureClass ENUTS2_2013.shp #;ENUTS3 FeatureClass ENUTS3.shp #;ENUTS3_2013 FeatureClass ENUTS3_2013.shp #"

Append geo1_ch1970_2011 ENUTS3 "Use the field map to reconcile field differences" "CNTRY_NAME "CNTRY_NAME" true false false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\switzerland\geo1_ch1970_2011.shp,CNTRY_NAME,0,50;CNTRY_CODE "CNTRY_CODE" true false false 4 Text 0 0,First,#,C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\switzerland\geo1_ch1970_2011.shp,CNTRY_CODE,0,4;BPL_CODE "BPL_CODE" true false false 19 Double 0 0,First,#;ENUTS3 "ENUTS3" true false false 10 Text 0 0,First,#;ADMIN_NAME "ADMIN_NAME" true false false 250 Text 0 0,First,#,C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\switzerland\geo1_ch1970_2011.shp,ADMIN_NAME,0,250" # #

CopyMultiple "C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\enuts\ENUTS2.shp shapefile_polygon;C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\enuts\ENUTS2_2013.shp shapefile_polygon;C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\enuts\ENUTS3.shp shapefile_polygon;C:\Users\heima033\Documents\ArcGIS\ipumsi\enuts\ENUTS3_2013.shp shapefile_polygon" Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts\zipped_shapefiles ENUTS2;ENUTS2_2013;ENUTS3;ENUTS3_2013 "ENUTS2 FeatureClass ENUTS2.shp #;ENUTS2_2013 FeatureClass ENUTS2_2013.shp #;ENUTS3 FeatureClass ENUTS3.shp #;ENUTS3_2013 FeatureClass ENUTS3_2013.shp #"

Append new_candidates_2024 ENUTS3 "Use the field map to reconcile field differences" "CNTRY_NAME "CNTRY_NAME" true false false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts
ew_candidates_2024.shp,CNTRY_NAME,0,254;CNTRY_CODE "CNTRY_CODE" true false false 4 Text 0 0,First,#,Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts
ew_candidates_2024.shp,CNTRY_CODE,0,4;BPL_CODE "BPL_CODE" true false false 19 Double 0 0,First,#,Z:\ipumsi\geography\gis\GIS_enuts
ew_candidates_2024.shp,BPL_CODE,-1,-1;ENUTS3 "ENUTS3" true false false 10 Text 0 0,First,#;ADMIN_NAME "ADMIN_NAME" true false false 250 Text 0 0,First,#" # #

cntAddress.delPoint: Minnesota Population Center, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South
Minnesota Population Center, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South

idCitation.otherCitDet: Citation: Minnesota Population Center. Integrated Public Use Microdata Series, International: Version 6.2 [Machine-readable database]. Minneapolis: University of Minnesota, 2013.

dataIdInfo.idAbs: ENUTS3 identifies the Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics (NUTS) within Europe. It is a standard administrative division of the European Union, and was developed by the EU. The European Free Trade Association extends the NUTS system to several additional countries outside of the EU, and they are also incorporated into this variable. NUTS3 identifies the third level territorial units within countries. Where officially-defined geographical units have populations less than 20,000, they have been combined with neighboring geographical units to create units with populations greater than 20,000.

Consts.useLimit: IPUMS International data are available free of charge, but their use imposes responsibilities upon the user. To access the data, a prospective user must submit an electronic authorization form identifying the user by name, electronic address, and institutional affiliation. All persons are granted a limited license to use and distribute this documentation and the available data, subject to the legal constraints listed below.IPUMS International boundary data are intended for use as visual representation and statistical analysis. The boundaries do not necessarily reflect exact political delineations.

LegConsts.useLimit: Authorization to use IPUMS International data constitutes a legal binding agreement between the applicant, the applicant's institution, the University of Minnesota, and the relevant official statistical authorities. Submitting false, misleading or fraudulent information constitutes a violation of this agreement. Misusing the data by violating any of the conditions detailed below also constitutes a violation of this agreement and may lead to professional censure, loss of employment, or civil prosecution under relevant national and international laws, and to sanctions against your institution, at the discretion of the University of Minnesota and the official statistical authorities.
LegConsts.othConsts: USAGE LICENCE: 1. User agrees to receive occasional email messages. 2. Data must not be redistributed without authorization. 3. Data are intended only for scholarly research and educational purposes. 4. Commercial use and redistribution of the data is strictly prohibited. 5. Scholarly publications are permitted, and must be cited appropriately. 6. User agrees to notify ipums@umn.edu regarding possible errors in the map.

dataIdInfo.idPurp: Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics (NUTS 3) for Austria, Greece, Ireland, Portugal, Romania, Slovenia, Spain, and Switzerland.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Funding provided by National Science Foundation and National Institutes of Health. Microdata provided by national statistical offices of the partner countries.

dataExt.exDesc: Geographic extent: Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics (NUTS) within Europe

cntAddress.delPoint: Minnesota Population Center, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South

Consts.useLimit:
Portugal: The following ENUTS 3 units were merged due to confidentiality

  • Pinhal Interior Norte (PT164 ), Pinhal Interior Sul (PT166), Serra da Estrela (PT167), Beira Interior Norte (PT168), Beira Interior Sul (PT169), and Cova da Beira (PT16A) –new ENUTS3 code 22169

  • Alentejo Litoral (PT181), Alto Alentejo (PT182), Alentejo Central (PT183), and Baixo Alentejo (PT184) –new ENUTS3 code 22189

Portugal: The following ENUTS 3 units were recoded

  • Oeste (PT 16B) –new ENUTS3 code 22166

  • Medio Tejo (PT 16C) –new ENUTS 3 code 22167

Spain: The islands along the east coast were recoded into 9999 due to missing data from IPUMS. They belong to Illes Balears (ES53). Their original codes from ENUTS document are:

  • ES531: Eivissa y Formentera

  • ES532: Mallorca

  • ES533: Menorca

Spain: The islands along the west coast of Morocco were recoded into 9999 due to missing data from IPUMS. They belong to Canarias (ES70). Their original codes from ENUTS document are:

  • ES703: El Hierro

  • ES704: Fuerteventura

  • ES705: Gran Canaria

  • ES706: La Gomera

  • ES707: La Palma

  • ES708: Lanzarote

  • ES709: Tenerife

Austria: The following ENUTS 3 units were merged due to confidentiality

  • Mittelburgenland (AT111) and Südburgenland (AT113) –new ENUTS3 code 1111

  • Lungau (AT321 ) and Pinzgau-Pongau (AT322) –new ENUTS3 code 1321

  • Außerfern (AT331) and Oberland (AT334) –new ENUTS3 code 1331

  • Osttirol (AT333) and Tiroler Unterland (AT335) –new ENUTS3 code 1333 –these two units are not adjacent to each other.

Switzerland: The following ENUTS 3 units were merged due to confidentiality

  • Appenzell Ausserrhoden (CH053 ) and Appenzell Innerrhoden (CH054) –new ENUTS3 code 34053

Greece: The northwestern peninsula of Aghion Oros (an autonomous polity within the Hellenic Republic under the official name of "Autonomous Monastic State of the Holy Mountain") is not listed as part of Greece on the ENUTS document. IPUMS combines it with Chalkidiki (GR127).

dataLineage.statement: International country boundaries for the world are available from the Office of the Geographer’s Large Scale International Boundary Lines and World Vector Shorelines (LSIB-WVS) polygon file. They are matched with maps available from EUROSTAT and Eurostat methologies and working paper, Nomenclature of territorial units for statistics, NUTS 2010/EU-27, 2011 edition (http://ec.europa.eu/eurostat).

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.
